title: CrewAI Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and techniques for using CrewAI effectively in engineering projects.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the AI's responses.
commands.scenario: When generating creative content or brainstorming ideas.

commands.description: Selects the advanced model for more complex tasks.
commands.scenario: When handling tasks requiring deep understanding and nuanced responses.

commands.description: Simplifies complex concepts into layman's terms.
commands.scenario: When explaining technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.

commands.description: Enables real-time streaming of responses.
commands.scenario: When building interactive applications that require immediate feedback.

commands.command: "Think step by step. Then verify your answer."
commands.description: Encourages the model to follow a logical process and check its work.
commands.scenario: When solving complex problems that require detailed reasoning.

commands.description: Limits the response length to manage cost and focus.
commands.scenario: When generating concise outputs for summaries or short answers.

commands.description: Increases the detail level in the AI's responses.
commands.scenario: When detailed explanations are necessary for understanding or documentation.

commands.command: "Use bullet points to list the steps."
commands.description: Formats the response in a structured, easy-to-read manner.
commands.scenario: When creating step-by-step guides or instructions.

commands.description: Sets a maximum time limit for the AI to generate a response.
commands.scenario: When integrating into systems with strict response time requirements.

commands.command: "Focus on the main idea and ignore minor details."
commands.description: Directs the AI to prioritize key points over minutiae.
commands.scenario: When summarizing lengthy documents or discussions.

commands.description: Enables detailed logging for troubleshooting and analysis.
commands.scenario: When diagnosing issues or optimizing AI performance.

commands.command: "Provide a counterargument to the following point."
commands.description: Generates an opposing viewpoint for balanced discussions.
commands.scenario: When preparing for debates or critical analysis.

commands.description: Authenticates API requests with your unique key.
commands.scenario: When setting up CrewAI for the first time in your application.

commands.command: "Summarize the following text in three sentences."
commands.description: Condenses information into a brief summary.
commands.scenario: When needing quick overviews of lengthy texts.

commands.description: Retries the request up to three times in case of failure.
commands.scenario: When ensuring reliability in unstable network conditions.

commands.description: Enables a manager agent to oversee the workflow, delegating tasks and validating outputs instead of a simple sequential flow.
A parameter within the Agent definition that permits the agent to pass tasks to other specialized agents in the crew.
Activates short-term, long-term, and entity memory, allowing agents to learn from previous interactions within the crew execution.
Forces the task output to conform to a specific Pydantic model or JSON schema for seamless downstream API integration.
Sets a rate limit (Requests Per Minute) to prevent API provider throttling and manage token consumption costs.
Enable local caching to prevent redundant LLM calls for identical tasks, reducing latency and API costs.
Explicitly assign a more capable model to the manager in hierarchical processes to improve orchestration logic.
Configure specific embedding models for the CrewAI memory system to improve RAG-based retrieval accuracy.
Pass a callback function to monitor and log every individual step taken by an agent for real-time debugging.
Define a precise schema in the task's expected_output to force the agent into a specific structural pattern.
